Flat pricing beats Substack's %.</p><h3 id="common-failure-patterns" tabindex="-1">Common Failure Patterns: </h3><p>These seem to be some of the themes.</p><ol><li><strong>Acquisition Neglect</strong>: Platforms bought for talent/users then abandoned (Posterous, Tumblr)</li><li><strong>Financial Unsustainability</strong>: Can't generate enough revenue to cover costs (Open Diary, Pownce)</li><li><strong>Feature Creep</strong>: Trying to be everything, mastering nothing (Pownce, Windows Live Spaces)</li><li><strong>Cultural Mismatch</strong>: New owners don't understand user base (Tumblr, LiveJournal)</li><li><strong>Technical Stagnation</strong>: Failure to innovate while competitors advance (Blogger, Xanga)</li><li><strong>User Hostile Decisions</strong>: Changes that alienate core users (Tumblr's NSFW ban, Medium's paywall)</li><li><strong>Legal/External Threats</strong>: Lawsuits or regulatory issues (Gawker)</li></ol><p>So there's this massive history of these platforms rising up to consume the users fleeing the previous failure. Only to then fall apart themselves years later.</p><h2 id="a-step-back" tabindex="-1">A step back </h2><p>Another way to make this point might be to ask why we needed these platforms in the first place? Why were there so many of these? And why did they do so well for so long?</p><p>It's because individual writers couldn't do one or more of the following:</p><ul><li>Host a blog where they could write</li><li>Send mass emails without getting blocked</li><li>Get eyeballs on their content</li></ul><p>Well, it's now a whole lot easier to do all three.</p><aside>Creating all these things with high quality and good security is still a bit difficult, but getting easier all the time.</aside><p>Creating a website that hosts a blog is fairly trivial for AI today, and it will only become more trivial in the months and years to come. So any would-be writer can simply have AI help build them a blog. And it won't even have to look exactly like all the other pages, which is a problem on a site like Substack, who wants to maintain its own branding above that of the creator.</p><p>Sending mass emails is still a bit of a dark art, but there are services like Amazon SES that AI could also use to build this infrastructure fairly easily. Not to mention using any of the other third-party services that are designed to help people do this. It just becomes a matter of integration with those tools, which AI is really good at.</p><p>And finally, the benefit of a platform for getting eyes onto your content I think is diminishing as well. First of all, it's not clear to me that anyone on Substack is just getting tons of traffic by virtue of just being there.</p><p>Second, I think sites like Google and other AI-powered platforms are going to get extremely good at finding the content jewels of what's publicly available and raising them up to others. Basically, AI-powered content discovery. And I think that will have far more impact at lifting the best content.</p><h3 id="we-can-just-do-it-ourselves" tabindex="-1">We can just do it ourselves </h3><p>The bottom line is, we're just going to be able to do these things on our own.</p><p>The platforms tend to be extremely toxic and highly restrictive, and they don't last long anyway. And the only reason they existed in the first place is because it was hard to do the things that they provided.</p><p>And now with AI, that is all going away.</p><div class="mt-4"><h4>Notes</h4><div class="text-sm font-concourse-t3 font-extralight text-gray-500"><!--[--><h4 id="platform-shutdown-sources" tabindex="-1">Platform Shutdown Sources </h4><ol><li><p><strong>Posterous</strong>: <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2013/02/15/posterous-will-shut-down-on-april-30th-co-founder-garry-tan-launches-posthaven-to-save-your-sites/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">TechCrunch - "Posterous Will Shut Down On April 30th"</a> (February 15, 2013)</p></li><li><p><strong>Windows Live Spaces</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://betanews.com/2010/09/29/microsoft-windows-live-spaces-already-dead-wordpress-com-will-only-get-1-of-30m-users/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">BetaNews - "Windows Live Spaces already dead"</a> (September 29, 2010)</li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Gawker</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://www.forbes.com/sites/mattdrange/2016/06/21/peter-thiels-war-on-gawker-a-timeline/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Forbes - "Peter Thiel's War On Gawker: A Timeline"</a></li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Tumblr</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://www.theverge.com/2019/3/14/18266013/tumblr-porn-ban-lost-users-down-traffic" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">The Verge - "Tumblr's porn ban lost it 30% of its traffic"</a> (March 14, 2019)</li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Medium</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://www.cjr.org/the_media_today/medium-has-pivoted-so-many-times-it-has-now-come-full-circle.php" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Columbia Journalism Review - "Medium has pivoted so many times it has now come full circle"</a></li><li><a href="https://blog.medium.com/renewing-mediums-focus-98f374a960be" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Medium - "Renewing Medium's focus"</a> (January 4, 2017)</li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Ghost</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://w3techs.com/technologies/details/cm-ghost" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">W3Techs - "Usage statistics of Ghost"</a></li><li><a href="https://ghost.org/pricing/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Ghost Pricing</a></li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Substack</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://www.platformer.news/why-platformer-is-leaving-substack/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Platformer - "Why Platformer is leaving Substack"</a> (January 11, 2024)</li><li><a href="https://variety.com/2022/digital/news/substack-layoffs-1235306249/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Variety - "Substack Layoffs: Newsletter Startup Cuts 14% of Staff"</a> (June 29, 2022)</li></ul></li><li><p><strong>Beehiiv</strong>:</p><ul><li><a href="https://techcrunch.com/2024/04/30/beehiiv-newsletters/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">TechCrunch - "beehiiv attracts $33M to make its newsletter publishing platform more sticky"</a> (April 30, 2024)</li><li><a href="https://www.howtheygrow.co/p/how-beehiiv-grows" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">How They Grow - "How beehiiv Grows"</a></li></ul></li></ol><!--]--></div></div></div>]]></content:encoded>
            <author>daniel@danielmiessler.com (Daniel Miessler)</author>
        </item>
        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Building a Personal API]]></title>
            <link>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/launching-daemon-personal-api?utm_source=rss&amp;utm_medium=feed&amp;utm_campaign=website</link>
            <guid>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/launching-daemon-personal-api</guid>
            <pubDate>Fri, 01 Aug 2025 18:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
            <description><![CDATA[Personal daemon visualization/images/personal-daemon-launch.png/images/personal-daemon-launch.png Super hyped to be launching the very early first version of _Daemon_ today Since 2014 I've been obsessed with this idea of everything having an API. I first talked about it in 2016 in my kind of crapp...]]></description>
            <content:encoded><![CDATA[<div><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/personal-daemon-launch.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/personal-daemon-launch.png" alt="Personal daemon visualization"></a></p><p>Super hyped to be launching the (very early) first version of <em>Daemon</em> today!</p><p>Since 2014 I've been obsessed with this idea of everything having an API. I first talked about it in 2016 in my (kind of crappy but with great ideas) book <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/the-real-internet-of-things">The Real Internet of Things</a>.</p><blockquote> So this is the first building block: every object has a daemonâ€”An API to the world that all other objects understand. Any computer, system, or even a human with appropriate access, can look at any other object's daemon and know precisely how to interact with it, what its status is, and what it's capable of. <cite><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Real-Internet-Things-Daniel-Miessler/dp/1545327122">The Real Internet of Things, 2016</a></cite></blockquote><p>The idea was that it wouldn't just be objectsâ€”like cars or restaurantsâ€”that got daemons/APIs, but also people.</p><blockquote> Most importantly, humans themselves will also have daemons, and we'll be moving through a world full of other daemons. Human daemons will hold all information about a person, compartmentalized based on type, sensitivity, access restrictions, etc., and that data will be used to send hyper-personalized requests to the daemons around us. <cite><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Real-Internet-Things-Daniel-Miessler/dp/1545327122">The Real Internet of Things, 2016</a></cite></blockquote><h2 id="combining-with-digital-assistants" tabindex="-1">Combining with Digital Assistants </h2><p><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/A-DA.avif" alt="A-DA"></p><p>And that then clicked with the other main concept, which was that we'd have AI-powered Digital Assistants (DAs) that would constantly process these thousands of APIs that were constantly "around" us, since there's no way we could do that as humans.</p><aside>I've been obsessed with this context piece forever.</aside><blockquote> The most visible and significant role that Synthetic Intelligence will play in the near future will be serving as the interface between humans and the world. <br><br>To clarify, I don't mean the ever-promised, conscious, and self-improving brand of SI that so much science fiction is based on. <br><br>The SI I'm referring to I define as: A computer system that can monitor human context, intentions, and commands, interpret them, and then take action as well as or better than a (human) professional personal assistant. <cite><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Real-Internet-Things-Daniel-Miessler/dp/1545327122">The Real Internet of Things, 2016</a></cite></blockquote><p>So the idea was that DAs would fundamentally change how we interact with the things around us using tech. Instead of us using our devices to do it, which doesn't scale, our DAs would be doing it for us.</p><blockquote> Instead of interacting with technology directly, we will interact with our DA, and our DA will work out the details with the necessary daemon. We speak, things happen. We gesture, things happen. We text, things happen. No need to find, understand, or master new techâ€”that's for the service and the DA to work out amongst themselves. <cite><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Real-Internet-Things-Daniel-Miessler/dp/1545327122">The Real Internet of Things, 2016</a></cite></blockquote><h2 id="how-i-think-it-ll-work-in-practice" tabindex="-1">How I think it'll work in practice </h2><p>So for people specifically, the use case I always think of is the coffee shop, where you're single, and your DA knows you're looking for a relationship, and you walk in and it reads all the daemons in the shop.</p><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/56388a12-d7d0-468c-9ee8-c1a61ad337e1-DA-profile-analysis-miessler.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/56388a12-d7d0-468c-9ee8-c1a61ad337e1-DA-profile-analysis-miessler.png" alt="Coffee shop daemon visualization"></a></p><p>You're waiting in line at Starbucks, and Kai (your DA) is continuously reading all the public Daemons (things) and Auras (people) around you. Kai lights up a girl in front of you because she matches on so many things.</p><ul><li>7/9 favorite reading match</li><li>Shy but loving in a relationship</li><li>Dogs &gt; Cats</li><li>ðŸ˜ She believes it should be legal to kill people who chew loudly</li></ul><p>So Kai starts talking to <em>her</em> DA, Tara, and now he and Tara are about to tell you two where to look so you see each other from across the room.</p><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/206d3cce-7088-458b-80ef-746f28e55e8e-aipp-coffee-aura-miessler.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/206d3cce-7088-458b-80ef-746f28e55e8e-aipp-coffee-aura-miessler.png" alt="Coffee aura visualization"></a></p>Even everyday objects will have their own auras<aside>I've haven't worked a lot on the data content yet, updates to follow.</aside><p>So, Daemon is my early version of thisâ€”a public endpoint that serves up-to-date information about me in a format that both humans and AIs can use.</p><h2 id="architecture" tabindex="-1">Architecture </h2><div class="mermaid-diagram" style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;"><div class="mermaid"></div></div>The Daemon architecture on the Cloudflare MCP<p>And here's a rough breakdown of how interactions work.</p><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/daemon-architecture.avif"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/daemon-architecture.avif" alt="Daemon MCP architecture on Cloudflare Workers"></a></p>Daemon MCP architecture on Cloudflare Workers (click for full size)<h2 id="how-to-use-it" tabindex="-1">How to Use It </h2><p>Daemon runs as an <a href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">MCP (Model Context Protocol)</a> server at <code>https://daemon.danielmiessler.com</code>. Here's how to interact with it:</p><h3 id="get-available-tools" tabindex="-1">Get Available Tools </h3><p>First, see what endpoints are available:</p><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">curl</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -X</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> POST</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> https://daemon.danielmiessler.com</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> \</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  -H</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "Content-Type: application/json"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> \</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  -d</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> '{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "jsonrpc": "2.0",</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "method": "tools/list",</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "id": 1</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">  }'</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br></div></div><p>This returns a list of all available tools:</p><div class="language-json line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">json</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "jsonrpc"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"2.0"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "result"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    "tools"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "name"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"get_about"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "description"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"Get basic information about Daniel Miessler"</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "name"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"get_telos"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "description"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"Get Daniel's TELOS framework - problems, missions, goals"</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">      // ... more tools</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    ]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "id"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br><span class="line-number">13</span><br><span class="line-number">14</span><br><span class="line-number">15</span><br><span class="line-number">16</span><br><span class="line-number">17</span><br></div></div><h3 id="call-a-tool" tabindex="-1">Call a Tool </h3><p>To get information from a specific endpoint, like my TELOS (purpose framework):</p><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">curl</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -X</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> POST</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> https://daemon.danielmiessler.com</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> \</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  -H</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "Content-Type: application/json"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> \</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  -d</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> '{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "jsonrpc": "2.0",</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "method": "tools/call",</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "params": {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">      "name": "get_telos",</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">      "arguments": {}</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "id": 2</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">  }'</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br></div></div><p>This returns my TELOS framework data:</p><div class="language-json line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">json</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "jsonrpc"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"2.0"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "result"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    "content"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "type"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"text"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">        "text"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"TELOS is my personal framework for tracking purpose and direction in life...</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">Problems (P):</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">- P1: People lack meaning in their lives...</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">- P2: People are stuck in a 1950's style hierarchical mindset...</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">Missions (M):</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">- M1: Increase human Eudaimonia...</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">- M2: Build systemsâ€”heavily leveraging AI..."</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    ]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "id"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">2</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br></div></div><h2 id="mcp-configuration" tabindex="-1">MCP Configuration </h2><p>If you want to add Daemon to your <a href="https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Claude Code</a> or other MCP-compatible tools, add this to your MCP config:</p><div class="language-json line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">json</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  "mcpServers"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    "daemon"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">      "url"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">: </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"https://daemon.danielmiessler.com"</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br></div></div><h2 id="available-endpoints" tabindex="-1">Available Endpoints </h2><p>Here's what you can query through Daemon:</p><aside>I'm still working on a lot of these. They might not all work properly yet.</aside><ul><li><code>get_about</code> - Basic information about me and what I do</li><li><code>get_narrative</code> - My personal narrative and focus areas</li><li><code>get_mission</code> - What I'm trying to accomplish</li><li><code>get_projects</code> - My current projects</li><li><code>get_telos</code> - My TELOS framework (Problems, Missions, Goals, Metrics)</li><li><code>get_favorite_books</code> - My favorite books</li><li><code>get_favorite_movies</code> - My favorite movies</li><li><code>get_current_location</code> - Where I am currently</li><li><code>get_preferences</code> - Personal preferences and work style</li><li><code>get_all</code> - Get all available data at once</li><li><code>get_section</code> - Get a specific section by name</li></ul><h2 id="what-s-next" tabindex="-1">What's Next </h2><p>This is version 0.1 of Daemon. I plan to expand it with more endpoints, real-time updates, and tons more. So many ideas.</p><p>I'm currently working on the ability to update my daemon via little voice notes from my iPhone, so like:</p><blockquote><p>Just landed in Vegas</p></blockquote><p>...and have that get added to the <code>timeline</code> and <code>location</code> sections of my daemon.</p><p>I'll also be putting out a full guide on how to set this up for yourselfâ€”or any other entity that you think needs an API.</p><p>More to come.</p><div class="mt-4"><h4>Notes</h4><div class="text-sm font-concourse-t3 font-extralight text-gray-500"><!--[--><ol><li><p>MCP stands for Model Context Protocol - it's Anthropic's new standard for AI agents to interact with external tools and APIs. Learn more at <a href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/">modelcontextprotocol.io</a>.</p></li><li><p>The daemon.md file updates are synced via a simple update script that parses the markdown and uploads to <a href="https://developers.cloudflare.com/kv/">Cloudflare KV</a>. And I can actually just give Kai (my digital assistant) verbal updates and he makes all the changes and pushes them within a couple of seconds!</p></li><li><p>Kai retrieved the header image from my <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/ai-predictable-path-7-components-2024">"AI's Predictable Path"</a> post and created all the diagrams you see in this post automatically based on the text. If you want to read more about that, check out my <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/personal-ai-infrastructure">personal AI infrastructure</a> post.</p></li><li><p>You can my (very short) <em>The Real Internet of Things</em> without dealing with Amazon by reading the <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/the-real-internet-of-things">blog post version</a> I recently published here in full on the site.</p></li></ol><!--]--></div></div></div>]]></content:encoded>
            <author>daniel@danielmiessler.com (Daniel Miessler)</author>
        </item>
        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Increased Worker Pressure from AI]]></title>
            <link>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/ai-worker-pressure?utm_source=rss&amp;utm_medium=feed&amp;utm_campaign=website</link>
            <guid>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/ai-worker-pressure</guid>
            <pubDate>Fri, 01 Aug 2025 17:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
            <description><![CDATA[AI overwork and pressure/images/ai-overwork.png/images/ai-overwork.png My latest depressing thought about AI is that with all the pressure to adopt AI and replace employees with automation, the lived experience of many/most remaining employees is likely to get far far worse. Iâ€™m imagining a whole...]]></description>
            <content:encoded><![CDATA[<div><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/ai-overwork.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/ai-overwork.png" alt="AI overwork and pressure" width="1370" height="1372" loading="eager" fetchpriority="high"></a></p><p>My latest depressing thought about AI is that with all the pressure to adopt AI and replace employees with automation, the lived experience of many/most <em>remaining</em> employees is likely to get far far worse.</p><p>Iâ€™m imagining a whole lot of overbearing managers making unreasonable demands and forgoing even the pretense of caring. Late hours, changed deadlines, and a general attitude of,</p><blockquote><p>Youâ€™re lucky you even have this job...</p></blockquote><p>Meanwhile, everyone in the company knows there are one or more departments working on how to replace them.</p><p>So itâ€™s not just the people who lose their jobs who will be suffering, but the people who still have them.</p><p>Itâ€™s gonna be a rough 5 to 10 years.</p></div>]]></content:encoded>
            <author>daniel@danielmiessler.com (Daniel Miessler)</author>
        </item>
        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Self-Contained TypeScript Programs Using Bun]]></title>
            <link>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/executable-typescript-programs-using-bun?utm_source=rss&amp;utm_medium=feed&amp;utm_campaign=website</link>
            <guid>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/executable-typescript-programs-using-bun</guid>
            <pubDate>Sun, 27 Jul 2025 19:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
            <description><![CDATA[Attempt-13/images/Attempt-13.png Bun installing dependencies automatically If you hate Python as much as me it"s probably because of dependencies. Roughly 23-319% of the time, when I run a Python app, it doesn't work because of dependencies, and I end up trying to figure out which of my 17 real an...]]></description>
            <content:encoded><![CDATA[<div><p><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/Attempt-13.png" alt="Attempt-13"></p>Bun installing dependencies automatically If you hate Python as much as me it"s probably because of dependencies. <p>Roughly 23-319% of the time, when I run a Python app, it doesn't work because of dependencies, and I end up trying to figure out which of my 17 real and virtual Python environments are actually active.</p><p>Using <code>uv</code> for everything is way better, but since I'm kind of moving my entire programming world to TypeScript, I'm now using <code>bun</code>'s auto-install feature instead. And it's actually a bit better.</p><h2 id="different-approaches-same-goal" tabindex="-1">Different Approaches, Same Goal </h2><p>Python's <code>uv</code> and Bun both solve the "self-contained app" problem by putting the requirements inside the program itself, but they do it in different ways...</p><h3 id="uv-s-approach-inline-comments-smuggling-basically" tabindex="-1">UV's Approach: Inline Comments (smuggling, basically) </h3><p>Python uses special magical comments to declare dependencies:</p><div class="language-python line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">python</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line highlighted"><span style="color:#6A737D;">#</span><span style="color:#6A737D;"> /// script</span></span>
<span class="line highlighted"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># dependencies = ["requests", "rich"]</span></span>
<span class="line highlighted"><span style="color:#6A737D;">#</span><span style="color:#6A737D;"> ///</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">import</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> requests</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># script continues...</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br></div></div><p>It works great, but it feels super hack-y to me.</p><p>It feels like we're smuggling in a <!----> dependency payload to trick Python into actually working for once.</p><h3 id="bun-s-approach-just-import-it-like-normal" tabindex="-1">Bun's Approach: Just import it like normal </h3><p>I like <code>bun</code>'s approach <em>much</em> better. It just writes the imports out like it's not embarrassed by them!</p><div class="language-typescript line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">typescript</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">#!/usr/bin/env bun</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Just import what you need - Bun auto-installs!</span></span>
<span class="line highlighted"><span style="color:#D73A49;">import</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> axios </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">from</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'axios'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">;</span></span>
<span class="line highlighted"><span style="color:#D73A49;">import</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> chalk </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">from</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'chalk'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">;</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.cyan.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">bold</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">ðŸš€ Bun Auto-Install Demo</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Fetch a random joke</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">try</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">yellow</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'Getting a random joke...'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> jokeResponse</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> await</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> axios.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">get</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'https://official-joke-api.appspot.com/random_joke'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">);</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> joke</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> jokeResponse.data;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">green</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">joke</span><span style="color:#032F62;">.</span><span style="color:#24292E;">setup</span><span style="color:#032F62;">}`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">blue</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">joke</span><span style="color:#032F62;">.</span><span style="color:#24292E;">punchline</span><span style="color:#032F62;">} ðŸ˜„</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">} </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">catch</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> (error) {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">red</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'Failed to fetch joke</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">v</span><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Fetch a random activity</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">try</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">yellow</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'Finding something to do...'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> activityResponse</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> await</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> axios.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">get</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'https://bored-api.appbrewery.com/random'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">);</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> activity</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> activityResponse.data;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">magenta</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`Activity: ${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">activity</span><span style="color:#032F62;">.</span><span style="color:#24292E;">activity</span><span style="color:#032F62;">}`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">dim</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`Type: ${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">activity</span><span style="color:#032F62;">.</span><span style="color:#24292E;">type</span><span style="color:#032F62;">} | Participants: ${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">activity</span><span style="color:#032F62;">.</span><span style="color:#24292E;">participants</span><span style="color:#032F62;">}</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">} </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">catch</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> (error) {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">red</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'Failed to fetch activity</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Show a random number to prove it runs fresh</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> randomNum</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> Math.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">floor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(Math.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">random</span><span style="color:#24292E;">() </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">*</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1000</span><span style="color:#24292E;">);</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">green</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">`Random number: ${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">randomNum</span><span style="color:#032F62;">}`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Show that this runs fresh each time</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">dim</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">Run again for different results!'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">console.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">log</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(chalk.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">dim</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'No package.json or npm install needed ðŸŽ‰</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">));</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br><span class="line-number">13</span><br><span class="line-number">14</span><br><span class="line-number">15</span><br><span class="line-number">16</span><br><span class="line-number">17</span><br><span class="line-number">18</span><br><span class="line-number">19</span><br><span class="line-number">20</span><br><span class="line-number">21</span><br><span class="line-number">22</span><br><span class="line-number">23</span><br><span class="line-number">24</span><br><span class="line-number">25</span><br><span class="line-number">26</span><br><span class="line-number">27</span><br><span class="line-number">28</span><br><span class="line-number">29</span><br><span class="line-number">30</span><br><span class="line-number">31</span><br><span class="line-number">32</span><br><span class="line-number">33</span><br><span class="line-number">34</span><br><span class="line-number">35</span><br><span class="line-number">36</span><br></div></div><h2 id="running-the-script" tabindex="-1">Running the Script </h2><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Make it executable</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">chmod</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> +x</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> test.ts</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Run it directly - dependencies auto-install!</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">./test.ts</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Or just use bun</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">bun</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> test.ts</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br></div></div><p>The first time you run the script, <code>bun</code> automatically:</p><ol><li>Detects the missing packages</li><li>Downloads and installs them</li><li>Caches them for future runs</li><li>Executes your script</li></ol><p>No <code>npm install</code>, no <code>package.json</code>, no setupâ€”just run it.</p><h2 id="example-output" tabindex="-1">Example Output </h2><div class="language- line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang"></span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span>ðŸš€ Bun Auto-Install Demo</span></span>
<span class="line"><span></span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Getting a random joke...</span></span>
<span class="line"><span></span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Did you watch the new comic book movie?</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>It was very graphic! ðŸ˜„</span></span>
<span class="line"><span></span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Finding something to do...</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Activity: Explore a park you have never been to before</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Type: recreational | Participants: 1</span></span>
<span class="line"><span></span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Random number: 294</span></span>
<span class="line"><span></span></span>
<span class="line"><span>Run again for different results!</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>No package.json or npm install needed ðŸŽ‰</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br><span class="line-number">13</span><br><span class="line-number">14</span><br><span class="line-number">15</span><br></div></div><p>Oh, and it's nuclear fast.</p><div class="language- line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang"></span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span>bun test.ts  0.08s user 0.06s system 29% cpu 0.469 total</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br></div></div><h2 id="my-takeaway" tabindex="-1">My takeaway </h2><p>This goes to a larger discussion around Python vs TypeScript, but I feel like this is another example where the latter is just a more natural, modern way of doing things.</p><p><em>TypeScript all the things.</em></p><div class="mt-4"><h4>Notes</h4><div class="text-sm font-concourse-t3 font-extralight text-gray-500"><!--[--><ol><li>Shoutout to Greg for getting me into the TypeScript cult.</li></ol><!--]--></div></div></div>]]></content:encoded>
            <author>daniel@danielmiessler.com (Daniel Miessler)</author>
        </item>
        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[I Built a Claude Code Context Modal Inside of Neovim]]></title>
            <link>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/neovim-claude-ai-plugin?utm_source=rss&amp;utm_medium=feed&amp;utm_campaign=website</link>
            <guid>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/neovim-claude-ai-plugin</guid>
            <pubDate>Sat, 26 Jul 2025 22:30:00 GMT</pubDate>
            <description><![CDATA[Kai Neovim AI Assistant/images/neovim-claude-ai-kai-update.png/images/neovim-claude-ai-kai-update.png Kai: AI-powered coding in Neovim click for full size I use LazyVim, btw. lol I've been using AI to help with coding for a while now, but going back and forth between my code and AI was getting t...]]></description>
            <content:encoded><![CDATA[<div><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/neovim-claude-ai-kai-update.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/neovim-claude-ai-kai-update.png" alt="Kai Neovim AI Assistant" width="2056" height="1988" loading="eager" fetchpriority="high"></a></p>Kai: AI-powered coding in Neovim (click for full size)<aside>I use LazyVim, btw. lol</aside><p>I've been using AI to help with coding for a while now, but going back and forth between my code and AI was getting tediousâ€”even with a highly optimized setup. So I integrated my Digital Assistant, Kai, directly into Neovim.</p><h2 id="the-problem" tabindex="-1">The Problem </h2><p>When working with AI for code editing, you typically want one of these actions:</p><ul><li><strong>Replace</strong> selected code with an improved version</li><li><strong>Insert</strong> new code based on context</li><li><strong>Display</strong> information without modifying anything</li></ul><aside>It's not perfect but I'm getting there.</aside><p>Most AI integrations make you tell them exactly what to do. Kai somewhat figures it out from how you ask.</p><h2 id="how-it-works" tabindex="-1">How It Works </h2><p>This entire thing is based on a capability in Claude Code that is <strong>massively</strong> underdiscussed. I just think of it as command-line mode.</p><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">claude</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -p</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br></div></div><ul><li>You can pipe into this thing</li><li>You could give it a string afterwards and it will just go and execute</li><li>You can even control how it uses different contexts and such.</li></ul><aside> Seriously, check the <a href="https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk">Claude Code SDK docs</a> It's ridiculous. </aside><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">claude</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -p</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "What's the weather like in San Francisco right now?"</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br></div></div><p>Anyway, that's what's going on under the hood. It's this command-line version of Claude Code that we're actually calling with this plug-in.</p><h3 id="structure" tabindex="-1">Structure </h3><p>The plugin has two main pieces:</p><ol><li><strong>Lua plugin</strong> (<code>kai-neovim.lua</code>) - Handles Neovim integration, visual selections, and buffer management</li><li><strong>Shell script</strong> (<code>kai-neovim.sh</code>) - Processes context and communicates with the AI backend</li></ol><aside>The shell script reads CLAUDE/AGENT.md files for project-specific context</aside><h3 id="smart-context-handling" tabindex="-1">Smart Context Handling </h3><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/Screenshot%202025-07-26%20at%2017.50.27.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/Screen-Shot-2013-09-12-at-9.24.54-PM.png" alt="Kai Progress Window" width="1920" height="1080" loading="lazy"></a></p>Kai's enhanced progress window showing detailed processing phases<p>The plugin always sends the entire buffer as context, but intelligently focuses on:</p><ul><li><strong>Selected text</strong> when you're in visual mode</li><li><strong>Cursor position</strong> when you're in normal mode</li></ul><p>This approach provides comprehensive context while enabling precise, targeted modifications based on your current selection or cursor position.</p><aside>Still working on polishing it.</aside><h3 id="intelligent-action-detection" tabindex="-1">Intelligent Action Detection </h3><p>The plugin lets you basically tell it anything, and it tries to work it out. Here are some examples.</p><ul><li>"Replace with X" â†’ Replaces selection</li><li>"Add a comment explaining" â†’ Inserts after selection</li><li>"What does this do?" â†’ Shows analysis in a popup</li><li>"Insert an appropriate image here." â†’ Creates a custom image and inserts it at that location</li></ul><!----><ul><li>"Fix the error" â†’ Replaces with corrected code</li></ul><p>This is just completely insane. We can just send arbitrary things and have it kind of figure it out. Basically, command-line interaction with AI. And within your text editor.</p><h2 id="the-code" tabindex="-1">The Code </h2><p>This is the basic code for it, but keep in mind it's a work in progress. It might be total garbage. And yes, I had Kai help me build it for sure.</p><h3 id="main-plugin-code" tabindex="-1">Main Plugin Code </h3><p>Save this as <code>~/.config/nvim/lua/kai-neovim.lua</code>:</p><div class="language-lua line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">lua</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> M </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {}</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Function to get visual selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">local</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> function</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> get_visual_selection</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get the visual selection marks</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> _, start_row, start_col, _ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getpos</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'&lt;"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> _, end_row, end_col, _ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getpos</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'&gt;"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get the lines</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_get_lines</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, start_row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, end_row, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> #</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 0</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    return</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ""</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Handle single line selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> #</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    lines[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">] </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> string.sub</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(lines[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">], start_col, end_col)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  else</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Multi-line selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    lines[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">] </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> string.sub</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(lines[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">], start_col)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> end_col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">&gt;</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 0</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      lines[</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">#</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines] </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> string.sub</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(lines[</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">#</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines], </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, end_col)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  return</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> table.concat</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(lines, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">end</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Function to escape special characters for shell</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">local</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> function</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> shell_escape</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(str)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  return</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "'" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> str</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">gsub</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "'"</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">end</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Main function to handle Kai Neovim integration</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">function</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> M</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">kai_enhance</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Set up subtle blue highlight for the input prompt</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'highlight KaiPrompt guifg=#e0e0e0 guibg=#1a1a2e'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get the prompt from user with custom highlighting</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'echohl KaiPrompt'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> prompt </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">input</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"ðŸ¤– Kai: "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'echohl None'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> prompt </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    print</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"No instruction provided."</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    return</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Check if we're in visual mode</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> mode </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">mode</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> is_visual </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> mode </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'v' </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">or</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> mode </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'V' </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">or</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> mode </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ''</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get selection if in visual mode, empty string otherwise</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> selection </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ""</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> is_visual </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    selection </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> get_visual_selection</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get current file path</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> filepath </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">expand</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'%:p'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get cursor position</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> cursor_row, cursor_col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_win_get_cursor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Get entire buffer content</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> buffer_content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> table.concat</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_get_lines</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">), </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Create a temporary file for the context</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> context_file </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> os.tmpname</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> f </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> io.open</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(context_file, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"w"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">  f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"CURRENT FILE: " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> filepath </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Always send the entire buffer</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">  f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"FULL BUFFER CONTENT:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> buffer_content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Add cursor position</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">  f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"CURSOR POSITION: Line " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> cursor_row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ", Column " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> cursor_col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> is_visual </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Include selection information when text is selected</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> _, start_row, start_col, _ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getpos</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'&lt;"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> _, end_row, end_col, _ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getpos</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"'&gt;"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">    f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"SELECTED TEXT (Lines " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> start_row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "-" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> end_row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "):</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> selection </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">    f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"MODE: User has selected specific text. Focus on this selection within the context of the entire buffer.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  else</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- When no selection, note cursor position</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">    f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"MODE: No selection. User's cursor is at line " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> cursor_row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ". Make targeted changes based on cursor location unless instructed otherwise.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">  f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">write</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"INSTRUCTION: " </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> prompt </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">  f</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">close</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Call Kai script</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> cmd </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> string.format</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">    "~/.config/nvim/scripts/kai-neovim.sh %s %s"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    shell_escape</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(context_file),</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    shell_escape</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(prompt)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  )</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Create progress notification (simplified for blog post)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  print</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"ðŸ¤– Processing with Kai..."</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Execute command</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> output </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">system</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(cmd)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Clean up temp file</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  os.remove</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(context_file)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Parse the action and content from the response</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(output, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, { plain </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> action </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> lines[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content_lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {}</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  for</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> i </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 2</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">#</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">do</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> lines[i] </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">~=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">or</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> i </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">&lt;</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> #</span><span style="color:#24292E;">lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">      table.insert</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(content_lines, lines[i])</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> table.concat</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(content_lines, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Remove any trailing newline</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> content</span><span style="color:#24292E;">:</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">gsub</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">$'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">''</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Handle different actions</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> action </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "[ACTION:DISPLAY]" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Create a floating window to display the analysis</span></span>
