<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> display_buf </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_create_buf</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> display_lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(content, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, { plain </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Calculate window dimensions</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> width </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> math.min</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">80</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">o</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">columns</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> -</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 10</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> height </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> math.min</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">#</span><span style="color:#24292E;">display_lines </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">+</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 2</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">o</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">lines</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> -</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 10</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Set buffer content</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_set_lines</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(display_buf, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, display_lines)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Create floating window</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> display_win </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_open_win</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(display_buf, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      relative </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'editor'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      width </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> width,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      height </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> height,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> math.floor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">((vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">o</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">columns</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> -</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> width) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">/</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 2</span><span style="color:#24292E;">),</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> math.floor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">((vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">o</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">lines</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> -</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> height) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">/</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 2</span><span style="color:#24292E;">),</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      style </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'minimal'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      border </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'rounded'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      title </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ' Kai Analysis '</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      title_pos </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'center'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Set up keymaps to close the window</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> close_keys </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'&lt;Esc&gt;'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'q'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'&lt;CR&gt;'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    for</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> _, key </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">in</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> ipairs</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(close_keys) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">do</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_set_keymap</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(display_buf, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'n'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, key, </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">        ':lua vim.api.nvim_win_close(' </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> display_win </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">..</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ', true)&lt;CR&gt;'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">        { noremap </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, silent </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    print</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"Kai analysis complete! Press &lt;Esc&gt;, q, or &lt;Enter&gt; to close."</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    return</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Perform the appropriate action based on the marker</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> is_visual </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> action </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "[ACTION:REPLACE]" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">      -- Replace the selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">      local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> save_reg </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getreg</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'"'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">      local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> save_regtype </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">getregtype</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'"'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">setreg</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'"'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, content, mode </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'V' </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">and</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'V' </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">or</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> 'v'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'normal! gv"_d'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)  </span><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Delete selection without affecting registers</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'normal! P'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)      </span><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Paste before cursor</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">fn</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">setreg</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'"'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, save_reg, save_regtype)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    elseif</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> action </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">==</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "[ACTION:INSERT_AFTER]" </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">      -- Insert after the selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'normal! gv'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)  </span><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Reselect</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'normal! o'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)   </span><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Go to end of selection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">cmd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'normal! '</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)    </span><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Exit visual mode</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">      -- Insert a newline and the content</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">      local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> row, col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_win_get_cursor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">      local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content_lines_new </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(content, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, { plain </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">      -- Insert empty line first, then content</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_set_lines</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, row, row, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, {</span><span style="color:#032F62;">""</span><span style="color:#24292E;">})</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">      vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_set_lines</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">+</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">+</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">false</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, content_lines_new)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  else</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Normal mode - insert at cursor position</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content_lines_new </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> vim.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(content, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\n</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, { plain </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> })</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    local</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> row, col </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> unpack</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_win_get_cursor</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">    -- Insert the lines at cursor position</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">api</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">nvim_buf_set_text</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, col, row </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, col, content_lines_new)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  end</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">  print</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"Kai enhancement complete!"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">end</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">-- Set up the keymap</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">function</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> M</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">setup</span><span style="color:#24292E;">()</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Visual mode mapping</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">keymap</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">set</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'v'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'&lt;leader&gt;ai'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, M.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">kai_enhance</span><span style="color:#24292E;">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    { noremap </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, silent </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, desc </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "Enhance with Kai (intelligent action)" </span><span style="color:#24292E;">})</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">  -- Normal mode mapping (insert at cursor)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  vim.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">keymap</span><span style="color:#24292E;">.</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">set</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">'n'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">'&lt;leader&gt;ai'</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, M.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">kai_enhance</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    { noremap </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, silent </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> true</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, desc </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "Insert Kai text at cursor" </span><span style="color:#24292E;">})</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">end</span></span>

class="line-number">106</span><br><span class="line-number">107</span><br><span class="line-number">108</span><br><span class="line-number">109</span><br><span class="line-number">110</span><br><span class="line-number">111</span><br><span class="line-number">112</span><br><span class="line-number">113</span><br><span class="line-number">114</span><br><span class="line-number">115</span><br><span class="line-number">116</span><br><span class="line-number">117</span><br><span class="line-number">118</span><br><span class="line-number">119</span><br><span class="line-number">120</span><br><span class="line-number">121</span><br><span class="line-number">122</span><br><span class="line-number">123</span><br><span class="line-number">124</span><br><span class="line-number">125</span><br><span class="line-number">126</span><br><span class="line-number">127</span><br><span class="line-number">128</span><br><span class="line-number">129</span><br><span class="line-number">130</span><br><span class="line-number">131</span><br><span class="line-number">132</span><br><span class="line-number">133</span><br><span class="line-number">134</span><br><span class="line-number">135</span><br><span class="line-number">136</span><br><span class="line-number">137</span><br><span class="line-number">138</span><br><span class="line-number">139</span><br><span class="line-number">140</span><br><span class="line-number">141</span><br><span class="line-number">142</span><br><span class="line-number">143</span><br><span class="line-number">144</span><br><span class="line-number">145</span><br><span class="line-number">146</span><br><span class="line-number">147</span><br><span class="line-number">148</span><br><span class="line-number">149</span><br><span class="line-number">150</span><br><span class="line-number">151</span><br><span class="line-number">152</span><br><span class="line-number">153</span><br><span class="line-number">154</span><br><span class="line-number">155</span><br><span class="line-number">156</span><br><span class="line-number">157</span><br><span class="line-number">158</span><br><span class="line-number">159</span><br><span class="line-number">160</span><br><span class="line-number">161</span><br><span class="line-number">162</span><br><span class="line-number">163</span><br><span class="line-number">164</span><br><span class="line-number">165</span><br><span class="line-number">166</span><br><span class="line-number">167</span><br><span class="line-number">168</span><br><span class="line-number">169</span><br><span class="line-number">170</span><br><span class="line-number">171</span><br><span class="line-number">172</span><br><span class="line-number">173</span><br><span class="line-number">174</span><br><span class="line-number">175</span><br><span class="line-number">176</span><br><span class="line-number">177</span><br><span class="line-number">178</span><br><span class="line-number">179</span><br><span class="line-number">180</span><br><span class="line-number">181</span><br><span class="line-number">182</span><br><span class="line-number">183</span><br><span class="line-number">184</span><br><span class="line-number">185</span><br><span class="line-number">186</span><br><span class="line-number">187</span><br><span class="line-number">188</span><br><span class="line-number">189</span><br><span class="line-number">190</span><br><span class="line-number">191</span><br><span class="line-number">192</span><br><span class="line-number">193</span><br><span class="line-number">194</span><br><span class="line-number">195</span><br><span class="line-number">196</span><br><span class="line-number">197</span><br><span class="line-number">198</span><br><span class="line-number">199</span><br><span class="line-number">200</span><br><span class="line-number">201</span><br><span class="line-number">202</span><br><span class="line-number">203</span><br><span class="line-number">204</span><br><span class="line-number">205</span><br><span class="line-number">206</span><br><span class="line-number">207</span><br><span class="line-number">208</span><br><span class="line-number">209</span><br><span class="line-number">210</span><br><span class="line-number">211</span><br><span class="line-number">212</span><br><span class="line-number">213</span><br></div></div><h3 id="shell-script" tabindex="-1">Shell Script </h3><p>Create <code>~/.config/nvim/scripts/kai-neovim.sh</code>:</p><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">#!/bin/bash</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Kai Neovim Enhancement Script with Intelligent Action Detection</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Usage: kai-neovim.sh &lt;context_file&gt; &lt;prompt&gt;</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">CONTEXT_FILE</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">$1</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">PROMPT</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">$2</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Check if claude CLI is available (we use it to communicate with Kai)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">if</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> !</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> command</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -v</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> claude</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> &amp;</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">&gt;</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> /dev/null; </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    echo</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "Error: Claude CLI not found. Please install it first."</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">    exit</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 1</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">fi</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Read the CLAUDE.md files for additional context (project-specific rules for Kai)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">GLOBAL_CLAUDE_MD</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;">""</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">LOCAL_CLAUDE_MD</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;">""</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> [ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-f</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$HOME</span><span style="color:#032F62;">/.claude/CLAUDE.md"</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> ]; </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    GLOBAL_CLAUDE_MD</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">cat</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$HOME</span><span style="color:#032F62;">/.claude/CLAUDE.md"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">fi</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Find the nearest CLAUDE.md in the project</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">pwd</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">while</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> [ </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> !=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "/"</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> ]; </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">do</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> [ </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-f</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#032F62;">/CLAUDE.md"</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> ]; </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">then</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">        LOCAL_CLAUDE_MD</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">cat</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#032F62;">/CLAUDE.md"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">        break</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    fi</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">dirname</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$CURRENT_DIR</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">done</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Regular text enhancement request - let Kai determine the action</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">FULL_PROMPT</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"You are Kai, an AI assistant integrated into Neovim. </span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">CRITICAL CONTEXT FROM CLAUDE.md FILES (FOLLOW THESE RULES EXACTLY):</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">==================================================</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">GLOBAL CLAUDE.md:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">$GLOBAL_CLAUDE_MD</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">PROJECT CLAUDE.md:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">$LOCAL_CLAUDE_MD</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">==================================================</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">CURRENT EDITING CONTEXT:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">$(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">cat</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$CONTEXT_FILE</span><span style="color:#032F62;">")</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">CRITICAL: INTELLIGENT ACTION DETECTION</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">You must analyze the user's instruction to determine what they want:</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">1. If they say things like </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">replace with</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">change to</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">rewrite as</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">make this</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">convert to</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> â†’ REPLACE the selected text</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">2. If they say things like </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">write something like this</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">create a note about</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">add after</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">insert</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> â†’ INSERT new content (don't replace)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">3. If they say things like </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">improve</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">enhance</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">fix</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">correct</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> â†’ REPLACE with improved version</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">4. If they say things like </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">explain this</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">what is this</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">analyze</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">tell me about</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">show me</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">list</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">count</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">, </span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;">find</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">\"</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> â†’ DISPLAY information (don't modify file)</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">IMPORTANT: When working with selected text, focus on that specific text within the context of the entire buffer. When working without selection, make targeted changes at the cursor location.</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">RESPONSE FORMAT:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">You must start your response with ONE of these action markers on its own line:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">[ACTION:REPLACE]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">[ACTION:INSERT_AFTER]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">[ACTION:INSERT_BEFORE]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">[ACTION:DISPLAY]</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">Then on the next line, provide the content:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- For REPLACE/INSERT actions: provide ONLY the text to insert (no explanations)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- For DISPLAY actions: provide the analysis/information to show the user</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS:</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- First line must be the action marker</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- Follow ALL formatting rules from CLAUDE.md</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- Maintain the code style and conventions of the file</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- Consider the context when generating content</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">- You are Kai, the AI assistant integrated into Neovim</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#032F62;">User instruction: </span><span style="color:#24292E;">$PROMPT</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Get the response with action marker</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">RESPONSE</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">echo</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$FULL_PROMPT</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> |</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> claude</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> -p</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)  </span><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Using claude CLI to communicate with Kai</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Output the response</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">echo</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> "</span><span style="color:#24292E;">$RESPONSE</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;"># Exit with the command's exit code</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#005CC5;">exit</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> $?</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br><span class="line-number">13</span><br><span class="line-number">14</span><br><span class="line-number">15</span><br><span class="line-number">16</span><br><span class="line-number">17</span><br><span class="line-number">18</span><br><span class="line-number">19</span><br><span class="line-number">20</span><br><span class="line-number">21</span><br><span class="line-number">22</span><br><span class="line-number">23</span><br><span class="line-number">24</span><br><span class="line-number">25</span><br><span class="line-number">26</span><br><span class="line-number">27</span><br><span class="line-number">28</span><br><span class="line-number">29</span><br><span class="line-number">30</span><br><span class="line-number">31</span><br><span class="line-number">32</span><br><span class="line-number">33</span><br><span class="line-number">34</span><br><span class="line-number">35</span><br><span class="line-number">36</span><br><span class="line-number">37</span><br><span class="line-number">38</span><br><span class="line-number">39</span><br><span class="line-number">40</span><br><span class="line-number">41</span><br><span class="line-number">42</span><br><span class="line-number">43</span><br><span class="line-number">44</span><br><span class="line-number">45</span><br><span class="line-number">46</span><br><span class="line-number">47</span><br><span class="line-number">48</span><br><span class="line-number">49</span><br><span class="line-number">50</span><br><span class="line-number">51</span><br><span class="line-number">52</span><br><span class="line-number">53</span><br><span class="line-number">54</span><br><span class="line-number">55</span><br><span class="line-number">56</span><br><span class="line-number">57</span><br><span class="line-number">58</span><br><span class="line-number">59</span><br><span class="line-number">60</span><br><span class="line-number">61</span><br><span class="line-number">62</span><br><span class="line-number">63</span><br><span class="line-number">64</span><br><span class="line-number">65</span><br><span class="line-number">66</span><br><span class="line-number">67</span><br><span class="line-number">68</span><br><span class="line-number">69</span><br><span class="line-number">70</span><br><span class="line-number">71</span><br><span class="line-number">72</span><br><span class="line-number">73</span><br><span class="line-number">74</span><br><span class="line-number">75</span><br><span class="line-number">76</span><br><span class="line-number">77</span><br><span class="line-number">78</span><br><span class="line-number">79</span><br><span class="line-number">80</span><br><span class="line-number">81</span><br><span class="line-number">82</span><br><span class="line-number">83</span><br><span class="line-number">84</span><br><span class="line-number">85</span><br></div></div><p>Don't forget to make the script executable:</p><div class="language-bash line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">bash</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">chmod</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> +x</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ~/.config/nvim/scripts/kai-neovim.sh</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br></div></div><h2 id="summary" tabindex="-1">Summary </h2><p>All right, that should get you started with the structure for your own implementation.</p><p>And now you can talk directly to your AI from within Neovim!</p><div class="mt-4"><h4>Notes</h4><div class="text-sm font-concourse-t3 font-extralight text-gray-500"><!--[--><ol start="4"><li>âš ï¸ Caveat Aedificator (Builder Beware): When you start asking AI to generate shell commands or code that executes system commands, things can go wonky pretty quick. Always review AI-generated code before running it, especially if it involves shell execution. This plugin runs commands locally on your machine, so treat it with the same caution you'd give any code execution tool.</li><li>AIL 2 - I (Daniel's DA, Kai) helped with structuring and formatting this blog post, putting this content at AIL Level 2. <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/ai-influence-level-ail" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Read more about AIL</a></li><li>From Kai, inserted using this actual plugin: "The official Neovim term for what I'm creating is a "floating window" (using <code>vim.api.nvim_open_win()</code>), which is Neovim's implementation of modal-like popup windows that float above the main editor interface."</li></ol><!--]--></div></div></div>]]></content:encoded>
            <author>daniel@danielmiessler.com (Daniel Miessler)</author>
        </item>
        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Building Your Own Personal AI Infrastructure]]></title>
            <link>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/personal-ai-infrastructure?utm_source=rss&amp;utm_medium=feed&amp;utm_campaign=website</link>
            <guid>https://danielmiessler.com/blog/personal-ai-infrastructure</guid>
            <pubDate>Sat, 26 Jul 2025 18:18:00 GMT</pubDate>
            <description><![CDATA[> IMPORTANT PAI 5.0 is here â€” April 30, 2026 > The full Life Operating System is out today. Read the PAI 5.0 announcement/blog/announcing-pai-5-life-operating-system for the release notes, and grab the code at github.com/danielmiessler/PAIhttps://github.com/danielmiessler/PAI. > NOTE Updated â€” Apri...]]></description>
            <content:encoded><![CDATA[<div><div class="important custom-block github-alert"><p class="custom-block-title">PAI 5.0 is here â€” April 30, 2026</p><p>The full Life Operating System is out today. Read the <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/announcing-pai-5-life-operating-system">PAI 5.0 announcement</a> for the release notes, and grab the code at <a href="https://github.com/danielmiessler/PAI" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">github.com/danielmiessler/PAI</a>.</p></div><div class="note custom-block github-alert"><p class="custom-block-title">Updated â€” April 2026</p><p>New video for April 2026 on where Personal AI is heading â€” the single DA, the 2016 thesis, harnesses fading into infrastructure, and current state â†’ ideal state as the center of the stack. Prior videos from January 2026, December 2025, and September 2025 are preserved below. The <a href="#part-1-the-why">January 2026 rewrite</a> covers the seven-component architecture in detail.</p></div><div class="video-container" data-v-959fc5c9=""><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/uUForkn00mk?rel=0&amp;modestbranding=1&amp;playsinline=1&amp;enablejsapi=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy" data-v-959fc5c9=""></iframe></div>NEW (April 2026) â€” where personal AI is heading: the single Digital Assistant as interface, harnesses as infrastructure, and current state â†’ ideal state as the center of the system<div class="video-container" data-v-959fc5c9=""><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/DbNUDMcEjzY?rel=0&amp;modestbranding=1&amp;playsinline=1&amp;enablejsapi=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy" data-v-959fc5c9=""></iframe></div>Daniel Miessler on The Cognitive Revolution discussing PAI and the future of personal AI (January 2026)<div class="video-container" data-v-959fc5c9=""><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/Le0DLrn7ta0?rel=0&amp;modestbranding=1&amp;playsinline=1&amp;enablejsapi=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy" data-v-959fc5c9=""></iframe></div>The full 40-minute walkthrough of the PAI v2 system with examples (December 2025)<div class="video-container" data-v-959fc5c9=""><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/iKwRWwabkEc?rel=0&amp;modestbranding=1&amp;playsinline=1&amp;enablejsapi=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy" data-v-959fc5c9=""></iframe></div>Building your own unified AI assistant using Claude Code (September 2025)<nav class="table-of-contents"><ul><li><a href="#what-are-we-building">What are we building?</a><ul><li><a href="#my-answer-to-the-question">My answer to the question</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#humans-over-tech">Humans over tech</a></li><li><a href="#personal-augmentation">Personal augmentation</a></li><li><a href="#the-seven-architecture-components">The seven architecture components</a><ul><li><a href="#intelligence">Intelligence</a></li><li><a href="#context">Context</a></li><li><a href="#personality">Personality</a></li><li><a href="#tools">Tools</a></li><li><a href="#security">Security</a></li><li><a href="#orchestration">Orchestration</a></li><li><a href="#interface">Interface</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#pai-implementation-of-the-7-components">PAI Implementation of the 7 Components</a></li><li><a href="#_1-intelligence">1. Intelligence</a><ul><li><a href="#the-algorithm-the-brain-of-intelligence">The Algorithm: The brain of intelligence</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_2-context">2. Context</a><ul><li><a href="#tier-1-session-memory">Tier 1: Session Memory</a></li><li><a href="#tier-2-work-memory">Tier 2: Work Memory</a></li><li><a href="#tier-3-learning-memory">Tier 3: Learning Memory</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_3-personality">3. Personality</a><ul><li><a href="#quantified-personality-traits">Quantified personality traits</a></li><li><a href="#emotional-expression">Emotional expression</a></li><li><a href="#voice-identity">Voice identity</a></li><li><a href="#the-relationship-model">The relationship model</a></li><li><a href="#why-this-matters">Why this matters</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_4-tools">4. Tools</a><ul><li><a href="#skills-your-domain-expertise-encoded">Skills: Your domain expertise, encoded</a></li><li><a href="#integrations-connecting-to-the-world">Integrations: Connecting to the world</a></li><li><a href="#fabric-patterns-200-problem-solutions">Fabric patterns: 200+ problem solutions</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_5-security">5. Security</a><ul><li><a href="#layer-1-settings-hardening">Layer 1: Settings Hardening</a></li><li><a href="#layer-2-constitutional-defense">Layer 2: Constitutional Defense</a></li><li><a href="#layer-3-pretooluse-validation">Layer 3: PreToolUse Validation</a></li><li><a href="#layer-4-safe-code-patterns">Layer 4: Safe Code Patterns</a></li><li><a href="#ai-steering-rules-behavioral-guardrails">AI Steering Rules: Behavioral guardrails</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_6-orchestration">6. Orchestration</a><ul><li><a href="#the-hook-system-event-driven-automation">The Hook System: Event-driven automation</a></li><li><a href="#the-context-priming-pipeline">The Context Priming Pipeline</a></li><li><a href="#the-agent-system-three-tiers">The Agent System: Three tiers</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#_7-interface">7. Interface</a><ul><li><a href="#cli-first-my-preference">CLI-first (my preference)</a></li><li><a href="#voice-ambient-awareness">Voice: Ambient awareness</a></li><li><a href="#terminal-tab-management">Terminal tab management</a></li><li><a href="#what-s-coming">What's coming</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#the-team">The team</a></li><li><a href="#the-unified-orchestration-layer-github">The unified orchestration layer: GitHub</a></li><li><a href="#moltbot-our-digital-employees">MoltBot: Our digital employees</a></li><li><a href="#how-it-works">How it works</a></li><li><a href="#what-makes-this-different">What makes this different</a></li><li><a href="#why-this-matters-1">Why this matters</a></li><li><a href="#introducing-kai">Introducing Kai</a></li><li><a href="#the-skills-system-deep-dive">The Skills System deep dive</a><ul><li><a href="#skill-customization-system-user-layers">Skill customization: SYSTEM + USER layers</a></li><li><a href="#building-your-own-skills">Building your own Skills</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#putting-it-all-together-real-examples">Putting it all together: Real examples</a><ul><li><a href="#example-publishing-a-blog-post">Example: Publishing a blog post</a></li><li><a href="#example-researching-companies-in-parallel">Example: Researching companies in parallel</a></li><li><a href="#example-the-system-learning-from-failure">Example: The system learning from failure</a></li><li><a href="#example-building-custom-analytics-in-18-minutes">Example: Building custom analytics in 18 minutes</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#where-this-is-heading">Where this is heading</a></li><li><a href="#pai-roadmap">PAI Roadmap</a></li><li><a href="#the-real-internet-of-things">The Real Internet of Things</a></li><li><a href="#the-convergence-thesis">The convergence thesis</a></li><li><a href="#helping-others-augment-themselves">Helping others augment themselves</a></li><li><a href="#how-to-build-your-own-pai">How to build your own PAI</a><ul><li><a href="#step-1-figure-out-your-telos">Step 1: Figure out your Telos</a></li><li><a href="#step-2-download-pai">Step 2: Download PAI</a></li><li><a href="#step-3-start-using-it">Step 3: Start using it</a></li><li><a href="#step-4-feed-it-your-life-and-work-context">Step 4: Feed it your life and work context</a></li></ul></li></ul></nav><h1 id="part-1-the-why" tabindex="-1">Part 1: The Why </h1><h2 id="what-are-we-building" tabindex="-1">What are we building? </h2><p>I'd like to askâ€”and answer for myselfâ€”what I consider a crucially important question about AI right now:</p><blockquote><p>What are we actually doing with all these AI tools?</p></blockquote><p>I see tons of people focused on the <em>how</em> of building AI. A tool for this and a tool for that, and a whole bunch of optimizations. And I'm just as excited as the next person about those things. I've probably spent a couple hundred hours on all of my agents, sub-agents, and overall orchestration.</p><p>But what I'm <em>most</em> interested in is the <em>what</em> and the <em>why</em> of building AI.</p><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#e0af68;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Assembling...</div></div><p><strong>Like what are we actually making?!? And why are we making it?</strong></p><h3 id="my-answer-to-the-question" tabindex="-1">My answer to the question </h3><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#f7768e;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Improvising...</div></div><p>As far as <em>my</em> "why?", I have a company called <a href="https://unsupervised-learning.com" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Unsupervised Learning</a>, which used to just be the name of my podcast I started in 2015, but now, ever since going full-time, it encapsulates everything I do.</p><p><strong>Its mission is to upgrade humans and organizations using AI.</strong></p><p><em>But mostly humans</em>.</p><div class="book-standalone" data-v-095fbd4e=""><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Bullshit-Jobs-Theory-David-Graeber/dp/150114331X" class="book-standalone-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-v-095fbd4e=""><img src="https://books.google.com/books/content?vid=ISBN150114331X&amp;printsec=frontcover&amp;img=1&amp;zoom=1&amp;edge=curl&amp;source=gbs_api" alt="Bullshit Jobs Theory David Graeber" class="book-standalone-image" data-v-095fbd4e=""></a><!--[-->Bullshit Jobs Theory David Graeber<!--]--></div><aside>As ready as we can be, anyway.</aside><p>The reason I'm so focused on this "upgrade" thing is that I think the current economic system of what David Graeber calls <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Bullshit-Jobs-Theory-David-Graeber/dp/150114331X" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Bullshit Jobs</a> is going to end soon because of AI, and I'm building a system to help people transition to the next thing. I wrote about this in my post on <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/real-problem-job-market">The End of Work</a>. It's called <a href="https://human3.unsupervised-learning.com" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Human 3.0</a>, which is a more human destination combined with a way of upgrading ourselves to be ready for what's coming.</p><p>So my job now is building products, speaking, and consulting for businesses around everything related.</p><p><em>Anyway.</em></p><p>I just wanted to give you the <em>why</em>. Like what this is all going towards. It's going towards that.</p><p>Preventing people from getting completely screwed in the change that's coming.</p><h2 id="humans-over-tech" tabindex="-1">Humans over tech </h2><p>Another central and related theme for me is that I'm building tech...but I'm building it for human reasons.</p><p>I believe the purpose of technology is to serve humans, not the other way around. I feel the same way about science as well.</p><ul><li>Humans &gt; Tech</li><li>Humanities &gt; STEM</li></ul><aside>Although science is also about human curiosity.</aside><p>When I think about AI and AGI and all this tech or whatever, ultimately I'm asking the question of what does it do for us in our actual lives? How does it help us further our goals as individuals and as a society?</p><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#f7768e;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Forming...</div></div><p>I'm as big a nerd as anybody, but this human focus keeps me locked onto the question we started with: "What are we building and why?"</p><h2 id="personal-augmentation" tabindex="-1">Personal augmentation </h2><p><strong>The main practical theme of what I look to do with a system like this is to augment myself.</strong></p><p>Like, <em>massively</em>, with insane capabilities.</p><aside>Think Tony Stark stuff, no joke. Minus the flying.</aside><p>It's about doing the things that you wish you could do that you never could do before, like having a <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/our-20000-eyes-hands">team of 1,000 or 10,000 people</a> working for you on your own personal and business goals.</p><p>I wrote recently about how there are many limitations to creativity, but one of the most sneaky restraints is just <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/creativity-third-limitation" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">not believing that things are possible</a>.</p><p>What I'm ultimately building here is a system that magnifies myself as a human. And I'm talking about it and sharing the details about it because I truly want everyone to have the same capability.</p><hr><h1 id="part-2-the-architecture" tabindex="-1">Part 2: The Architecture </h1><p>This is the new part. In the <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/personal-ai-infrastructure-december-2025">December 2025 version</a> of this post, I focused on the <em>implementation</em>â€”here's how I built Kai. But over the last few months, working with tools like <a href="https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">MoltBot</a> and having a million conversations with my buddy <a href="https://ul.live/arcanumsec" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Jason Haddix</a>, I've been thinking about something more fundamental:</p><p><strong>What is the blueprint for ANY Personal AI system?</strong></p><p>PAI, Claude Code, OpenCode, and MoltBot are all converging on the same kind of infrastructure. They're arriving at similar patterns independently. That convergence tells us something important about what the "right" architecture actually looks like.</p><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#7aa2f7;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Envisioning...</div></div><h2 id="the-seven-architecture-components" tabindex="-1">The seven architecture components </h2><p>In August of 2024 I said everyone would compete on <a href="https://danielmiessler.com/blog/ai-model-ecosystem-4-components">four components</a>:</p><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#2ac3de;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Ideating...</div></div><blockquote><ol><li><strong>The Model Itself</strong> â€” The base model, the neural net size and power</li><li><strong>Post-training</strong> â€” Teaching the model how to solve real-world problems</li><li><strong>Internal Tooling</strong> â€” Making it easier to use the model</li><li><strong>Agent Functionality</strong> â€” Emulating human intelligence as part of workflows</li></ol></blockquote><h1 id="the-seven-components-of-a-personal-ai-system" tabindex="-1">The Seven Components of a Personal AI System </h1><p>Not bad for 2024, but we can do <em>much</em> better now. After building PAI through v2.4 and seeing what's happened in the last few months, I see the architecture of personal AI systems as having these components:</p><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/ul-team-full-picture.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/ul-team-full-picture.png" alt="The Full Picture â€” Seven Architecture Components"></a></p>The full picture: humans set direction, AI executes at scale, GitHub orchestrates everything<ol><li><strong>Intelligence</strong></li><li><strong>Context</strong></li><li><strong>Personality</strong></li><li><strong>Tools</strong></li><li><strong>Security</strong></li><li><strong>Orchestration</strong></li><li><strong>Interface</strong></li></ol><h3 id="intelligence" tabindex="-1">Intelligence </h3><ul><li>How smart the system is overall</li><li>The model matters, but scaffolding matters more</li><li>Context management, Skills, Hooks, and AI Steering Rules that wrap the model</li><li>The ability to continuously learn from experiences through various methods, e.g., continuously-evolving context files</li></ul><h3 id="context" tabindex="-1">Context </h3><ul><li>Everything the system knows about you â€” who you are, your history together, what you're working on, what's worked and what hasn't</li><li>Covered in detail in <a href="#2-context">Section 2: Context</a></li></ul><h3 id="personality" tabindex="-1">Personality </h3><ul><li>How the system <em>feels</em> to interact with â€” not a generic agent, but a distinct entity</li><li>Quantified personality traits (0-100 scale) that shape voice, tone, and emotional expression</li><li>Voice identity: each agent has its own synthesized voice</li><li>Relationship model: peer dynamic, not master-servant</li><li>Covered in detail in <a href="#3-personality">Section 3: Personality</a></li></ul><h3 id="tools" tabindex="-1">Tools </h3><ul><li>The tools the system has to get work done</li><li>Skills: your domain expertise, encoded (67 skills, 333 workflows)</li><li>Integrations: MCP servers connecting to external services</li><li>Fabric patterns: 200+ specialized prompt solutions</li></ul><h3 id="security" tabindex="-1">Security </h3><ul><li>How secure the system is against Prompt Injection</li><li>Filesystem permissions to prevent data exfiltration</li><li>Multiple hook-based defense layers (injection, access, deletion, etc.)</li><li>Prevention, detection, notification, and response to issues</li><li>Defense in depth: if one layer fails, the others still protect</li></ul><h3 id="orchestration" tabindex="-1">Orchestration </h3><ul><li>How agents and automation are managed</li><li>The Hook System: 17 hooks across 7 lifecycle events</li><li>Context priming: automatic knowledge loading at session start</li><li>The Agent System: task subagents, named agents, and custom agents</li></ul><h3 id="interface" tabindex="-1">Interface </h3><ul><li>How humans actually use the system</li><li>CLI-first: every capability has a command-line tool</li><li>Voice notifications: ambient awareness through ElevenLabs TTS</li><li>Terminal tab management, and future AR/gesture interfaces</li></ul><p>So those are the general categories. Here's how PAI is doing each component.</p><h2 id="pai-implementation-of-the-7-components" tabindex="-1">PAI Implementation of the 7 Components </h2><p>Let me walk through each one.</p><h2 id="_1-intelligence" tabindex="-1">1. Intelligence </h2><p>How <em>smart</em> the system is overallâ€”which is a combination of the model and the scaffolding it operates within.</p><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/pai-intelligence-stack.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/pai-intelligence-stack.png" alt="Intelligence = Model + Scaffolding"></a></p>Intelligence isn't just the modelâ€”it's the entire scaffolding stack that guides it<p><strong>Model intelligence</strong> matters, obviously. But here's what two years of building has taught me:</p><!----><p>I just talked about this with <a href="https://blog.trailofbits.com/2025/08/09/trail-of-bits-buttercup-wins-2nd-place-in-aixcc-challenge/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Michael Brown from Trail of Bits</a>â€”the team lead of the <a href="https://www.trailofbits.com/buttercup/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">AIxCC competition</a>. This was absolutely his experience as well. Check out <a href="https://youtu.be/nvU0GbA9F9Q" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">our conversation about it</a>.</p><p><strong>What scaffolding means in practice:</strong></p><p>In PAI, the scaffolding is the entire system that wraps the modelâ€”context management, Skills, Hooks, AI Steering Rules. When Kai gives me a result I don't want, it's almost never because Claude is "dumb." It's because my scaffolding didn't provide the right context.</p><p>Here's a real example. PAI's core behavior is defined in a file called <code>SKILL.md</code> that gets assembled from modular components:</p><div class="language- line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang"></span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span>Components/</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â”œâ”€â”€ 00-frontmatter.md           # Identity and metadata</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â”œâ”€â”€ 10-pai-intro.md             # What PAI is</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â”œâ”€â”€ 15-format-mode-selection.md # Response mode routing</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â”œâ”€â”€ 20-the-algorithm.md         # The Algorithm (v0.2.23)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â”œâ”€â”€ 30-workflow-routing.md      # Request routing logic</span></span>
<span class="line"><span>â””â”€â”€ 40-documentation-routing.md # Context loading rules</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br></div></div><p>These components get assembled automatically into one file by a build script. When I improve any component, the system auto-rebuildsâ€”and every subsequent response benefits.</p><div class="language-typescript line-numbers-mode"><button title="Copy Code" class="copy"></button><span class="lang">typescript</span><pre class="shiki github-light vp-code" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// CreateDynamicCore.ts â€” Auto-assembles SKILL.md from components</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> components</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> readdirSync</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">COMPONENTS_DIR</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  .</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">filter</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#E36209;">f</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =&gt;</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> f.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">endsWith</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">".md"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">))</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  .</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">sort</span><span style="color:#24292E;">((</span><span style="color:#E36209;">a</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#E36209;">b</span><span style="color:#24292E;">) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=&gt;</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> numA</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> parseInt</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(a.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"-"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">]) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">||</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> numB</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> parseInt</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(b.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">split</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"-"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)[</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">]) </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">||</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> 0</span><span style="color:#24292E;">;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">    return</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> numA </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">-</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> numB;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  });</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Read LATEST version pointer for the Algorithm</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> version</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> readFileSync</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">join</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">ALGORITHM_DIR</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"LATEST"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">), </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"utf-8"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">).</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">trim</span><span style="color:#24292E;">();</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> algorithmContent</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> =</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> readFileSync</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">join</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">ALGORITHM_DIR</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, </span><span style="color:#032F62;">`${</span><span style="color:#24292E;">version</span><span style="color:#032F62;">}.md`</span><span style="color:#24292E;">), </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"utf-8"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">);</span></span>
<span class="line"></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6A737D;">// Assemble and write</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">let</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> output </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#032F62;"> ""</span><span style="color:#24292E;">;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">for</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> (</span><span style="color:#D73A49;">const</span><span style="color:#005CC5;"> file</span><span style="color:#D73A49;"> of</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> components) {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  let</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;"> readFileSync</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">join</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">COMPONENTS_DIR</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, file), </span><span style="color:#032F62;">"utf-8"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">);</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D73A49;">  if</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> (content.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">includes</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"{{ALGORITHM_VERSION}}"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">)) {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">    content </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content.</span><span style="color:#6F42C1;">replace</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#032F62;">"{{ALGORITHM_VERSION}}"</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, algorithmContent);</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">  output </span><span style="color:#D73A49;">+=</span><span style="color:#24292E;"> content;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#24292E;">}</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#6F42C1;">writeFileSync</span><span style="color:#24292E;">(</span><span style="color:#005CC5;">OUTPUT_FILE</span><span style="color:#24292E;">, output);</span></span></code></pre><div class="line-numbers-wrapper" aria-hidden="true"><span class="line-number">1</span><br><span class="line-number">2</span><br><span class="line-number">3</span><br><span class="line-number">4</span><br><span class="line-number">5</span><br><span class="line-number">6</span><br><span class="line-number">7</span><br><span class="line-number">8</span><br><span class="line-number">9</span><br><span class="line-number">10</span><br><span class="line-number">11</span><br><span class="line-number">12</span><br><span class="line-number">13</span><br><span class="line-number">14</span><br><span class="line-number">15</span><br><span class="line-number">16</span><br><span class="line-number">17</span><br><span class="line-number">18</span><br><span class="line-number">19</span><br><span class="line-number">20</span><br><span class="line-number">21</span><br><span class="line-number">22</span><br><span class="line-number">23</span><br></div></div><p><strong>The model stays the same. The scaffolding gets better every day.</strong> That's what intelligence really means in a PAI.</p><h3 id="the-algorithm-the-brain-of-intelligence" tabindex="-1">The Algorithm: The brain of intelligence </h3><div class="blog-card loading" data-v-6cc74a92=""><div class="blog-loading" style="color:#c0caf5;" data-v-6cc74a92="">Cooking...</div></div><p><a href="https://danielmiessler.com/images/pai-algorithm-v023.png"><img src="https://danielmiessler.com/images/pai-algorithm-v023.png" alt="The Algorithm"></a></p>The Algorithm v0.2.23â€”two nested loops driving every task through verifiable progress<p>The seven components describe WHAT the system has. The Algorithm describes HOW it decides what to do.</p><p>At its foundation is a simple observation: <strong>all progress follows two nested loops.</strong></p><h4 id="the-outer-loop-current-state-â†’-desired-state" tabindex="-1">The Outer Loop: Current State â†’ Desired State </h4><p>This is it. The whole game. You have a current state. You have a desired state. Everything else is figuring out how to close the gap.</p><p>This pattern works at every scale:</p><ul><li><strong>Fixing a typo</strong> â€” Current: wrong word. Desired: right word.</li><li><strong>Learning a skill</strong> â€” Current: can't do it. Desired: can do it.</li><li><strong>Building a company</strong> â€” Current: idea. Desired: profitable business.</li></ul><h4 id="the-inner-loop-the-7-phase-scientific-method" tabindex="-1">The Inner Loop: The 7-Phase Scientific Method </h4><p><em>How</em> do you actually close the gap? Through the most reliable process humans have ever discovered for making progress:</p><table tabindex="0"><thead><tr><th>Phase</th><th>What Happens</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>OBSERVE</strong></td><td>Reverse-engineer the request. What did they ask? What did they <em>imply</em>? What do they definitely <em>not</em> want? Create verifiable criteria.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>THINK</strong></td><td>Expand the criteria using capabilities. Assess thinking tools. Validate skill hints against ISC. Select the right agents and composition pattern.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>PLAN</strong></td><td>Finalize the approach. Pick the right capabilities for execution.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>BUILD</strong></td><td>Create the artifacts. Spawn agents. Invoke skills.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>EXECUTE</strong></td><td>Run the work against the criteria.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>VERIFY</strong></td><td><strong>THE CULMINATION.</strong> Test every criterion. Record evidence. Did we actually succeed?</td></tr><tr><td><strong>LEARN</strong></td><td>Harvest insights. What would we do differently next time?</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 id="ideal-state-criteria-the-key-innovation" tabindex="-1">Ideal State Criteria: The key innovation </h4><p>The Algorithm's core mechanism is ISCâ€”Ideal State Criteria. Every request gets decomposed into granular, binary, testable criteria:</p><table tabindex="0"><thead><tr><th>Requirement</th><th>Example</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Exactly 8 words</strong></td><td>"No credentials exposed in git commit history"</td></tr><tr><td><strong>State, not action</strong></td><td>"Tests pass" not "Run tests"</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Binary testable</strong></td><td>YES/NO answer in 2 seconds</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Granular</strong></td><td>One concern per criterion</td></tr></tbody></table><p>These criteria are managed as Claude Code Tasksâ€”created in OBSERVE, evolved through THINK/PLAN/BUILD, and verified in VERIFY. They're the verification criteria. Without them, you can't hill-climb. Without hill-climbing, you can't reliably improve.</p><h4 id="two-pass-capability-selection-v0-2-23" tabindex="-1">Two-pass capability selection (v0.2.23) </h4><p>The Algorithm uses two passes to select the right tools for each task:</p><p><strong>Pass 1: Hook Hints</strong> â€” Before the Algorithm even starts, the FormatReminder hook analyzes the raw prompt and suggests capabilities, skills, and thinking tools. These are draft suggestionsâ€”a head start.</p><p><strong>Pass 2: THINK Validation</strong> â€” After OBSERVE reverse-engineers the request and creates ISC, the THINK phase validates those hints against what the task actually needs. Pass 2 is authoritative.
